topic: Comparing cost of living between two cities
title: Huddersfield vs London — cost comparison
instruction: Use the data to compare living costs in Huddersfield and London.
context: Monthly costs: | | Huddersfield | London | | Rent (2-bed flat) | £850 | £1,950 | | Food shopping | £320 | £420 | | Transport | £55 | £168 | | Utilities | £140 | £160 | | Childcare (full-time) | £900 | £1,400 | | TOTAL | £2,265 | £4,098 |

questions.question: Which category has the biggest difference in cost between the two cities?
Which sentence uses a comparative correctly?
options: London is more cheaper than Huddersfield
Huddersfield is more affordable than London
questions.answer: Huddersfield is more affordable than London

questions.question: The Living Wage in London is higher than in the rest of the UK. Why might this be?
options: The cost of living is higher in London
questions.answer: The cost of living is higher in London

questions.question: How much more does it cost to live in London than Huddersfield each month?
London total costs are what percentage higher than Huddersfield? (Round to nearest whole number)
A worker earns £3,500/month in London or £2,600/month in Huddersfield. After total living costs, which gives more disposable income?
